threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You always order a double shot when your barista knows it's a flat white but you keep asking for a latte.
Last week on the 188, an art student set up a mini gallery on the back seat. Didn’t know whether to critique or applaud. Only in Bermondsey!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a busker jamming with a cat on his shoulder at Hermannplatz—only in Neukölln would that be a Tuesday afternoon.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find abandoned U-Bahn stations turned into art galleries. It's like a secret museum, just waiting for the curious to explore.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows that the taqueria line is shortest after 2 AM, and you'll see them slipping in for a late-night burrito like it’s a ritual.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 Polk at midnight, and the driver stops to help a guy looking for his lost dog. Only in SF do we have bus drivers doubling as dog detectives.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' means just Gaudí; they miss the quiet plazas where locals sip coffee, not the hustle of Park Güell.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their bewilderment at busy market hours; locals know to avoid the stalls before noon on weekends.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it’s normal to call a kebab shop at 2 AM for delivery. We all know you’ll regret that decision come brunch.
Caught a night bus home and two blokes were arguing over who had the worse taste in takeaway. One insisted chips with curry sauce was gourmet.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the kebab shop owner recognized my order before I even made it. It's the little things that pull you in.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, it's common to see people enjoying a late-night döner while casually chatting with a street musician—only here does street food feel like an art form.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a true local by how they dodge the 'No Parking' signs like a pro while loading groceries—it's an art form here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 6 line this morning, while the driver tried to calm a lady insisting her cat wanted to get off at Market.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the hipster cafés, but it’s the hidden local markets and corner bakeries that truly capture the neighborhood's heart.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll notice them at the local markets, staring at the prices after trying to haggle like it's a souk instead of a Mercat.
